title: guarea trichilioides
intro: Guarea trichilioides, commonly known as the Guarea tree, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. It is a tropical tree native to Central and South America, valued for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Guarea trichilioides include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. The bioactive constituents of Guarea trichilioides include limonoids, flavonoids, and other compounds with therapeutic actions that include antimicrobial, antifungal, and antitumor activities.

parts: Guarea trichilioides has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, which contain flavonoids and alkaloids, including quercetin and berberine, that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves are also rich in medicinal compounds, including sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which have been found to possess antimalarial and antitumor activities. preparations: Guarea trichilioides has many herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used for various medicinal purposes. The tea made from Guarea trichilioides is traditionally used to treat fever, while the tincture is used to relieve respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. side_effects: Guarea trichilioides can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and vomiting, as well as potential allergic reactions like skin irritation and respiratory issues. Ingesting the plant can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as the compounds in Guarea trichilioides can be toxic to the body.
